summary: Fair audience recording captures an emotionally charged indoor show featuring a standout Black Peter jam, a magical Help‑Slip sequence and moving renditions of Morning Dew and Touch of Grey.
review: The performance is remembered for its raw emotion – Jerry’s voice was raw, belting choruses and even shedding tears on songs like Morning Dew, So Many Roads and Touch of Grey. Reviewers highlight a powerful second set with Help‑Slip and an unforgettable Black Peter jam, while noting the lack of a soundboard source makes the audio only fair but still listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers gave 5‑star praise for the show’s emotional intensity and standout songs, while the recording is noted as an audience capture with only fair fidelity; weighted average yields a solid 4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No soundboard source; listeners note lower fidelity and some missing detail, but overall the tape is usable for enjoying the performance.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: An indoor summer‑tour show that felt especially intimate; the first set was steady while the second set delivered a magical Help‑Slip sequence and an explosive Black Peter jam, ending with deeply emotional ballads.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as not feeling well but delivering heartfelt vocals, belting choruses and even shedding tears on several songs; his performance is called magical despite health concerns.
